venue: Frost Bank Center, San Antonio, TX

bet_audit.position: OKC +3.5 via No: SAS wins by over 3.5

phase_analysis.Q1: SAS opened 35-22 with 8 made threes in 14 attempts and Wembanyama spacing OKC out of normal coverage. Castle produced early connective passing and the Spurs led wire-to-wire.
phase_analysis.Q2: OKC won 31-25 and cut the game to 60-53, but the recovery was bench/role driven, not SGA driven. This created the false recovery signal.
phase_analysis.Q3: The decisive segment: SAS 32, OKC 13. OKC went 6/28 FG and 0/8 from three while SAS created 14 free throw attempts and won the quarter by 19.
phase_analysis.Q4: Garbage-time state. SAS maintained control, OKC starters were pulled or limited, and the spread was dead.

diagnostic_findings: SAS shot quality and spacing were sustainable enough to outlast early variance label
OKC shot quality was not terrible at 0.53 but conversion collapsed: 42.11 eFG and 24.39 non-heave 3PT
SAS had similar shot quality at 0.54 but much better conversion: 54.49 eFG and 59.00 TS
Rebounding tilted SAS: tracking total 58-48 and Wembanyama/Kornet structure created interior control despite Holmgren counting 11 boards
OKC main creator suppression was confirmed: SGA 15 points on 6/18 and 0/5 from three
Jalen Williams limitation created rotation instability and weakened OKC secondary creation
Q2 recovery should not have overridden Q1 structural warning
game7_prep_directive: Game 7 must start from a recalibrated series prior. Do not auto-bet OKC revenge without pricing SAS structural spacing, Wembanyama high-leverage rim/3PT duality, Castle creation, OKC wing-health limitation, and third-quarter collapse risk. Require market edge after our own projected line.
